_status: SIGNED-OFF 2026-07-13 — owner-authorized go-live (3rd batch). Class I (American Whitewater rates the corridor Class I-II; upper reaches are the liveliest)
name: War Eagle Creek
difficultyRating: Class I (American Whitewater rates the corridor Class I-II; upper reaches are the liveliest)
description: A scenic Class I Ozark float in northwest Arkansas, flowing past Withrow Springs State Park and the historic War Eagle Mill to Beaver Lake. Framed by tall limestone bluffs; a rain-dependent, flashy creek best floated during spring runoff.
managingAuthority: Arkansas State Parks (Withrow Springs State Park); Arkansas Game and Fish Commission (AGFC — fisheries/access, led the 2023 dam-removal project); U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (Beaver Lake and the lower backwater). No single river-wide manager.
accessLaw: Arkansas: the public may float and fish navigable/floatable streams and use the streambed and water below the ordinary high-water mark; War Eagle Creek is a recognized floatable stream. Streambanks and adjacent land are private — put in / take out only at public road crossings, Withrow Springs State Park, or private outfitter access.

gauges.name: War Eagle Creek near Hindsville, AR

putIn.name: Hwy 23 upper access near the fish hatchery / St. Paul-Boston area, SE Madison County

sections.name: Hwy 412 to Withrow Springs State Park (Hwy 23)

takeOut.name: Withrow Springs State Park / Hwy 23 bridge

sections.name: Withrow Springs State Park (Hwy 23) to Hwy 45 (Hindsville)

takeOut.name: Hwy 45 bridge near Hindsville (at USGS gauge 07049000)
War Eagle Mill / War Eagle bridge, above the Beaver Lake backwater (Benton County)

research.calibrationKeySource: Withrow Springs SP / AR Own Backyard / Arkansas Canoe Club gage-height float key (optimal band, 4 sources); danger omitted (single-source)
research.calibrationNotes: UNIT: gage height feet (USGS 00065), current NAVD88 datum — the only unit every float key uses ('2.0-3.5 ft on the Hwy 45 gauge'; '2-3 ft at the USGS gauge'). The 'Hwy 45 gauge' IS USGS 07049000 (the Hwy 45 crossing at/near Hindsville); this section's take-out is at the gauge. Live confirm 2026-07-13 12:00 CDT: 1.98 ft / 276 cfs (USGS IV + USACE HDGA4), consistent with task (~2.01 ft / ~283 cfs) — today the creek sits right at the low/floatable floor (typical summer low). CFS CROSS-REFERENCE (approx, current rating): 2.0 ft≈280 cfs, 2.3 ft≈360 cfs (snoflo). A ladder was NOT built in cfs because sourced cfs values are unreliable: American Whitewater prose says '2.3'/120cfs (bare minimum) up to ~3.5'/800' but 2.3 ft is ~360 cfs today, so the 120-cfs figure is old-datum/typo — only the FEET values are internally consistent 2007-2026. TWO 'runnable' framings exist: casual FLOAT (outfitters/state park/on-water reports = 2.0-3.5 ft ideal, this ladder) vs WHITEWATER play (AW machine 'recommended' ~4 ft; whatsrunning.billdurr.com lists min 3 ft / optimal 6 ft) which target higher, pushier water — those higher whitewater numbers were EXCLUDED as inappropriate for a general float app and because they conflict with the ~4 ft flood line. FLASHY: creek spiked to ~2,200 cfs (10-day peak) in the days before 2026-07-13 and receded ~83%/day; flood of record ~40,500 cfs — a rapidly rising gauge is the real danger regardless of absolute stage. No official NWS/USACE flood-stage number could be sourced for this point.

hazards.name: War Eagle Mill low-head dam
hazards.description: Low dam/weir at the historic War Eagle Mill (War Eagle community, Benton County, just above the Beaver Lake backwater). MUST be portaged; steep banks on both sides make it a difficult portage. Present and in place as of 2026.

hazards.name: Low-water bridges / concrete slabs
hazards.description: County crossings such as Gar Hole Road ('the Slab') and others — only an inch or two of water over the slab at low flow (scrapy/exposed), but they form dangerous strainers/hydraulics at high water. Also commonly used as put-in/take-out access.

hazards.name: Strainers / log jams
hazards.description: Typical Ozark-creek wood hazard; the channel can be fully blocked in places, occasionally requiring portages of up to ~100 yards. Worse after high-water events.

hazards.name: Flash flooding
hazards.description: Flashy, rain-dependent creek that rises fast — spiked to ~2,200 cfs (10-day peak) just before 2026-07-13 and receded ~83%/day; flood of record ~40,500 cfs. Do not float during or right after heavy rain; a rapidly rising gauge is the primary danger regardless of absolute stage. Flood stage is 'just over 4 ft' on the Hwy 45 gauge.

hazards.name: Summer low water
hazards.description: Below ~2 ft on the gauge expect frequent dragging/wading, especially in canoes; the creek is often too low to float from mid-to-late summer into fall.

hazards.name: Removed hazard (historical) — upper-reach concrete dam
hazards.description: A ~12-ft-high low-head concrete dam ~1.5 miles east of Huntsville (Hwy 23-to-Hwy 412 upper reach) was REMOVED Nov 1-6, 2023 by an AGFC-led restoration project; older guidebooks and American Whitewater still list it as a required portage. A nearby overflow structure was also slated for removal.

hazards.name: Withrow Springs SP access closure (logistics)
hazards.description: As of recent reports, Withrow Springs State Park has NO War Eagle Creek access or parking due to Hwy 23 bridge construction; use private outfitters (e.g., OAR War Eagle Kayak & Campground on Hwy 23; Float Eureka) for put-in/shuttle. Verify before a trip.
